Skip to contents
clerkR 0.1.0
  • Get started
  • Reference
    • Getting started with clerkR
    • Archetypes
    • Descriptive tables (Table 1)
    • Simple descriptive tables
    • Correlation and partial correlation tables
    • Regression coefficient tables
    • Heritability tables with R-itable and clerkR

Package index

Table constructors

Build publication-ready tables from data frames.

tbl_descriptive()
Descriptive summary table with group comparisons (Table 1)
tbl_simple()
Simple descriptive summary table (no inferential tests)
tbl_correlation()
Correlation / partial correlation table
tbl_regression()
Regression coefficients table
tbl_heritability()
Heritability and variance components table

Render

Render a clerk_tbl to gt, HTML, or LaTeX.

clerk_render()
Render a clerk_tbl to its target output format
render_gt()
Render a clerk_tbl as a gt table (Word / PDF)
render_reactable()
Render a clerk_tbl as an interactive HTML table
render_latex()
Render a clerk_tbl as a LaTeX table

Options

Session-level formatting defaults — p-value style, decimal places, FDR display.

clerk_options()
clerkR session options

Theme & palette

clerkR visual identity — colours, scales, and gt theme.

clerk_theme()
Apply the clerkR gt theme to a gt table
clerk_palette()
clerkR colour palette
clerk_colour()
Access a single clerkR colour by name
clerk_diverging()
Build a clerkR diverging colour scale
clerk_sequential()
Build a clerkR sequential colour scale

Data

Synthetic example datasets.

clerk_example
Synthetic example dataset for clerkR
clerk_cor_example
Synthetic partial correlation results for clerkR examples
clerk_reg_example
Synthetic regression results for clerkR examples
clerk_h2_example
Synthetic heritability estimates for clerkR examples

Package

Package-level documentation.

clerkR clerkR-package
clerkR: Publication-Ready Tables for Biomedical Research

On this page

clerkR — Circadia Lab, Northumbria University. Lucas França.
